title: Learning Mechanisms Path
description: Build fluency in Learning Mechanisms inside Neural Networks by moving from foundational terms into adjacent methods, variants, and extensions.

steps.title: Spike-timing-dependent Plasticity (STDP)
steps.summary: A type of synaptic plasticity found in biological neurons, often used in spiking neural networks. It sits inside Neural Networks / Learning Mechanisms. Start after Spike-Timing Dependent Plasticity.
steps.whyIncluded: Start here to anchor the vocabulary and core intuition for this area.

steps.title: Spike-Timing Dependent Plasticity
steps.summary: Spike-Timing Dependent Plasticity belongs to the Neural Networks area of AI/ML, within Learning Mechanisms. It sits inside Neural Networks / Learning Mechanisms. Compare it with Spike-timing-dependent Plasticity (STDP) and Synaptic Plasticity. It opens into Spike-timing-dependent Plasticity (STDP).
steps.whyIncluded: Use this after the foundations to connect patterns, variants, and system behavior.

steps.title: Dependent Variable
steps.summary: Dependent Variable belongs to the Neural Networks area of AI/ML, within Learning Mechanisms. It sits inside Neural Networks / Learning Mechanisms. Compare it with Spike-Timing Dependent Plasticity and Spike-timing-dependent Plasticity (STDP).
steps.whyIncluded: Use this after the foundations to connect patterns, variants, and system behavior.

steps.title: Synaptic Plasticity
steps.summary: Synaptic Plasticity belongs to the Neural Networks area of AI/ML, within Learning Mechanisms. It sits inside Neural Networks / Learning Mechanisms. Compare it with Spike-Timing Dependent Plasticity and Spike-timing-dependent Plasticity (STDP).
steps.whyIncluded: Treat this as an advanced extension once the earlier terms feel natural.
